<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kashii-gū]][[de:Kashii-gū]][[en:Kashii-gū]][[fr:Kashii-gū]][[ja:香椎宮]][[zh:香椎宮]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kashii-gū]][[de:Kashii-gū]][[en:Kashii-gū]][[fr:Kashii-gū]][[ja:香椎宮]][[zh:香椎宮]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kashii-gū]][[de:Kashii-gū]][[en:Kashii-gū]][[fr:Kashii-gū]][[ja:香椎宮]][[zh:香椎宮]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Kashii-gū]][[en:Kashii-gū]][[fr:Kashii-gū]][[ja:香椎宮]][[zh:香椎宮]]


{{Userspace draft}}{{Expand French|Kashii-gū}}{{Expand Japanese|香椎宮}}{{Overly detailed}}{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|bang colspan="2" class="infobox-header" style="background-color:
#A12F25; color: #FFF;" |Kashii-gū<br /><br />香椎宮
|
|-
| colspan="2" class="infobox-below" style="background-color: #EBEBEB;" |
|image = File:Kashii gu0901.jpg
|caption=shrine|coordinates={{Coord|33.6534857|N|130.4526579|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|-
|Religion
|
|- class="note"
|bang class="infobox-label" scope="row" |{{ill|List of religions and spiritual traditions|en|List of religions and spiritual traditions|qid=Q329442|lt=Affiliation|1=List of religions and spiritual traditions}}
| class="infobox-data" |[[Shinto]]
|-
|[[Kami|Deity]]
|deity={{ill|Chūai|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}
|-
|Type
|Type = [[Chokusaisha]]<br /><br />[[Kanpei Taisha|Kanpei-taisha]]<br /><br />[[Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Jinja]]
|-
|location
|
|-
|location
|4-16-1, Kashii, {{ill|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}<br /><br />{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} 813-0011
|-
|<templatestyles src="Module:location map/styles.css"></templatestyles><div class="center"><div class="locmap" style="width:240px;float:none;clear:both;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto"><div style="width:240px;padding:0"><div style="position:relative;width:240px">[[File:Japan_location_map_with_side_map_of_the_Ryukyu_Islands.svg|class=notpageimage|240x240px|Kashii-gū is located in Japan]]<div class="od" style="top:77.061%;left:10.522%"><div class="id" style="left:-10px;top:-10px">[[File:Shinto_torii_icon_vermillion.svg|link=|class=notpageimage|20x20px|Kashii-gū]]</div></div></div><div style="padding-top:0.2em">Shown within Japan</div></div></div></div>
|-
|{{ill|Geographic coordinate system|en|Geographic coordinate system|qid=Q22664|lt=Geographic coordinates|1=Geographic coordinate system}}
|<templatestyles src="Module:coordinates/styles.css"></templatestyles><span class="geo-inline"><span class="plainlinks nourlexpansion">[https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Kashii-g%C5%AB&params=33_39_13_N_130_27_10_E_region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki <span class="geo-default"><span class="geo-dms" title="maps, aerial photos, and other data for this location"><span class="latitude">33°39′13″N</span> <span class="longitude">130°27′10″E</span></span></span><span class="geo-multi-punct">&#xFEFF; / &#xFEFF;</span><span class="geo-nondefault"><span class="geo-dec" title="maps, aerial photos, and other data for this location">33.65361°N 130.45278°E</span><span style="display:none">&#xFEFF; / <span class="geo">33.65361; 130.45278</span></span></span>]</span></span><indicator name="coordinates"><span id="coordinates">{{ill|Geographic coordinate system|en|Geographic coordinate system|qid=Q22664|lt=coordinates|1=Geographic coordinate system}}: <templatestyles src="Module:coordinates/styles.css"></templatestyles><span class="plainlinks nourlexpansion">[https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Kashii-g%C5%AB&params=33_39_13_N_130_27_10_E_region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki <span class="geo-default"><span class="geo-dms" title="maps, aerial photos, and other data for this location"><span class="latitude">33°39′13″N</span> <span class="longitude">130°27′10″E</span></span></span><span class="geo-multi-punct">&#xfeff; / &#xfeff;</span><span class="geo-nondefault"><span class="geo-dec" title="maps, aerial photos, and other data for this location">33.65361°N 130.45278°E</span><span style="display:none">&#xfeff; / <span class="geo">33.65361; 130.45278</span></span></span>]</span></span></indicator>
|-
|Architecture
|
|-
|[[Architectural style|Style]]
|type = kashii-zukuri
|architecture_type=kashii-zukuri|-
|Founder
|founder= Empress Jingū
|-
|Date established
|724
|-
|Website
|
|-
|<span class="url">[http://kashiigu.com/ kashiigu.com]</span>
|-
|[[File:Icon_of_Shinto.svg|link=|25x25px]] [[Glossary of Shinto]]
||prefecture={{ill|Kashii|qid=Q123365946|1=Kashii}}|architecture_style={{ill|Kashii-zukuri|qid=Q11667652|1=Kashii-zukuri}}}}
{{Nihongo|'''Kashii-gū'''|香椎宮|}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It worships {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}.<ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide">{{Cite web|title=Kashii-gu Shrine|url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/spot/12512|access-date=2024-03-28|website=VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>

It is one of the Three Great Shrines of Northern Kyushu, alongside [[Usa Jingū]], and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://www.amazon.ca/Mountain-Mandalas-Shugendo-Allan-Grapard/dp/1474249000|title=Mountain Mandalas: Shugendo in Kyushu|last=Grapard|first=Allan G.|last2=Rambelli|first2=Fabio|date=2016-02-25|publisher=Bloomsbury Academic|isbn=978-1-4742-4900-3|edition=Illustrated edition|location=London New York (N. Y.)|language=English}}</ref>{{rp|19}}

It was formerly in the countryside in a dense forest of about three hectares and now in the suburbs of the city of {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} in Japan. There are venerated Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} and his wife {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, whose son {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} is the fifteenth emperor of Japan, as well as the {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}} , sea deities..<ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp" /><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" /> 

As {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} found his final resting place here, older texts use the term ''Kashii-{{ill|Miao shrine|en|Miao shrine|qid=Q29043583|lt=byō|1=Miao shrine}}'' ({{Lang|ja|香椎廟}}, " Kashii courtyard ", " Kashii mausoleum ").<ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp">{{Cite web|title=HISTORY|url=http://www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp/J/kokubun/kashii/rekishi.html|access-date=2024-03-28|website=www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp}}</ref>

Since the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, the shrine has been part of the small group of shrines called ''[[chokusaisha]]'' which on certain specific occasions receive the {{Nihongo|envoys|勅使|Chokushi}} of the ''{{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=tennō|1=Emperor of Japan}}'' . As at the [[Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman-gū]] shrine (northeast of [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] ), this happens every ten years in Kashii. The 800m long staircase which leads to the sanctuary is lined with {{ill|Camphor|en|Camphor|qid=Q181559|lt=camphor trees|1=Camphor}} .

The structure, called {{Nihongo|Kashii style|香椎造|Kashii-zukuri}},<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kashii-zukuri (香椎造)|url=http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/k/kashiizukuri.htm|access-date=19 novembre 2019|website=www.aisf.or.jp|language=en}}.</ref> of the main building, (the ''[[honden]]'' ) — whose current appearance dates back to the last reconstruction in 1801 — can only be found here. The building is divided into three parts with hinged doors and a roof with many forks ( ''{{ill|Chigi (architecture)|en|Chigi (architecture)|qid=Q262282|lt=chigi|1=Chigi (architecture)}}'' ).<ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp-3">{{Cite web|title=HONDEN|url=http://www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp/J/kokubun/kashii/honden.html|access-date=2024-03-28|website=www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp}}</ref><ref name="now-2014" /><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" />

For many generations, the priesthood was reserved for four families who took turns every four years in the exercise of their functions.

Until the middleof the {{ill|20th century|en|20th century|qid=Q6927|lt=20th century|1=20th century}} there were in the vicinity a number of Buddhist temples of the [[Shingon school]] which, as a result of the law of " separation of Shinto and Buddhism » ( ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' ,{{Lang|ja|神仏分離}}) of 1868 were destroyed. Today, the only remaining witness to this past where Shintoism and Buddhism were mixed ( ''[[Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu shūgō]]'' ,{{Lang|ja|神仏習合|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}), is a small temple of the divinity [[Benzaiten]] .

== Historical Background ==
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingu|1=Empress Jingū}} founded the Kashii gu Shrine around the year 200 AD to honor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chuai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, her late husband, who is said to have passed away at this location. The shrine serves as a memorial for not Empress Jingu but their son, Emperor Ojin commemorating three important figures from ancient Japanese tales. While historical records do not definitively confirm their existence the shrine has maintained a connection to Japans lineage.<ref name="now-2014">{{Cite web|last=now|first=fukuoka|date=2014-05-28|title=Kashii-gu: A Shrine with Close Ties to the Imperial Court|url=http://www.fukuoka-now.com/en/kashii-gu-a-shrine-with-close-ties-to-the-imperial-court/|access-date=2024-03-28|website=Fukuoka Now|language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Let's travel around Japan">{{Cite web|title=Kashiigu shrine (Fukuoka city, Fukuoka Prefecture) - Let's travel around Japan!|url=http://www.travel-around-japan.com/k91-01-07-kashiigu.html|access-date=2024-03-28|website=Let's travel around Japan}}</ref><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" />

On the site are other small shrines dedicated to the deities {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}}, Takeuchi Sukune, etc. According to tradition, the legendary Jingū had a Kashii no miya shrine ({{Lang|ja|橿日宮}}) built here in {{ill|200|en|200|qid=Q23813|lt=200|1=200}}, when Chūai died in battle against the [[Kumaso]] people. There would have been an ancient sanctuary in the vicinity of this one but there is no historical mention of it before the year {{ill|723|en|723|qid=Q23545|lt=723|1=723}} .<ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp" /><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" />

Records indicate that the shrine was officially established in 724 following an oracle associated with Empress Jingû. Documentation from 728 further confirms the shrines existence during that era emphasizing its importance since its inception. Kashiigu has been acknowledged as a shrine evidenced by visits from imperial messengers every ten years—a tradition upheld throughout centuries.<ref name="Let's travel around Japan" />

A notable tradition that highlights this bond is the visitation by an envoy to Kashii gu Shrine once every ten years. This custom places Kashii gu among a group of 17 shrines across Japan that receive esteemed visits. Over time 138 imperial envoys have visited the shrine. The {{ill|Sandō|lt=|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sandō|qq=}} path leading to the shrine is called "Imperial Envoy Road " and it is lined with 165 camphor trees symbolizing unity among present municipalities, within the prefecture.<ref name="now-2014" /> This is because the shrine is a [[Chokusaisha]].<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=ZPUlAQAAIAAJ|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto: Norman Havens, Nobutaka Inoue. Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University, 2006.|accessdate=1 January 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Shinto Jiten (Dictionary of Shinto: 神道事典): Kokugakuin Daigaku Nihon Bunka Kenkyujo (国学院大学日本文化研究所), ed. Tokyo: Kokubundo, 1994.|url=https://www.amazon.com/Shinto-jiten-Japanese-Edition/dp/4335160232|accessdate=1 January 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Jinja jiten (神社辞典)/ Shirai Eiji ; Toki Masanori hen, 1997.|url=http://catalogue.nla.gov.au/Record/70123|accessdate=1 January 2014}}</ref>

== Facilities ==

=== Main hall ===
The main hall of Kashii gu showcases the style known as {{Nihongo|Kashii style|香椎造|Kashii-zukuri}}, a design specific, to this shrine. Constructed in 1801 under the patronage of Nagayori Kuroda, the lord of Chikuzen Province the current structure is believed to be a scaled down version of its form. Despite this the shrine continues to exude a sense of spaciousness and spiritual significance.<ref name="now-2014" /><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" /><ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp-3" />

There is a sacred [[Shinboku]] tree Ayasugi, a Japanese cedar tree whose seed was buried in the ground by {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingu|1=Empress Jingū}} herself.<ref name="now-2014" /><ref name="Let's travel around Japan" />

The gateway to the area is marked by the Romon gate leading to the core shrine complex. This complex comprises the Honden, Haiden, and Heiden all interconnected and reconstructed in 1801. The architectural style of the shrine, known as "{{Nihongo|Kashii style|香椎造|Kashii-zukuri}}" is distinct and specific to Kashiigu Shrine.<ref name="Let's travel around Japan" /><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" />

In addition, to its hall, Kashii gu houses many smaller Shrines or Setsumatsusha honoring various deities. Among them is a shrine that worships [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], a figure known for his longevity spanning five imperial reigns. .<ref name="now-2014" /><ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp-2">{{Cite web|title=FUROSUI|url=http://www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp/J/kokubun/kashii/furosui.html|access-date=2024-03-28|website=www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp}}</ref>

=== Spring of youth ===
The Furôsui Spring (water of youth)<ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" /> lies 500 meters northeast of the shrine within an area. According to legend a [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] who dRank from this spring lived for over three centuries. The water, from this spring is renowned for its purity. Is annually offered to the family during New Years festivities. Visitors can also sample this water at the shrine believed to possess life prolonging qualities.<ref name="Let's travel around Japan" /><ref name="VISIT FUKUOKA -Fukuoka Prefecture Official Travel Guide" /><ref name="now-2014" /><ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp-2" /> Until the [[Meiji Restoration]] there was an annual event of delivering water from it to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}.<ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp" /> [[Takenouchi no Sukune]] is said to have lived for centuries from drinking this water<ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp-2" />. The well is recognized as one of Japans 100 springs.<ref name="now-2014" /><ref name="www.ip.kyusan-u.ac.jp-2" /> 

The shrines grounds cover an area 400x200 meters with the former being the [[:en:X axis|X axis]] or west to east, and the latter or 200 meters being the [[:en:Y axis|Y axis]] of north to south. The western part of the precinct includes an approach spanning 120 meters. Moreover there is a pathway stretching one kilometer on the west side of the JR railway station lined with camphor trees leading to the shrines entrance.<ref name="Let's travel around Japan" />

There is a garden nearby full of Iris flowers.<ref name="Let's travel around Japan" />

==Gallery==
<gallery mode="packed">
File:Kashii-gu0903.jpg|The '''[[Honden]]''' or main shrine ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})
File:Back view of honden of Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Back view of honden|Back view of Honden
File:Cannon of Settsu in Kashii Shrine 2.JPG|alt=Cannon of Settsu|Cannon of Settsu
File:Cannon of Settsu in Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Cannon of Settsu|Cannon of Settsu
File:Chokushikan Hall of Kashii Shrine 2.jpg|alt=Chokushikan Hall|Chokushikan Hall
File:Chokushikan Hall of Kashii Shrine 20150223.JPG|alt=Chokushikan Hall|Chokushikan Hall
File:Chokushikan Hall of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Chokushikan Hall|Chokushikan Hall
File:Chozuya and Emado Hall of Kashii Shrine 20150223.JPG|alt=Chozuya and Emado Hall|Chozuya and Emado Hall
File:Direction Sign of Former Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Direction Sign|Direction Sign
File:Drum in Kaguraden Hall of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Drum in Kaguraden Hall|Drum in Kaguraden Hall
File:Ema on hamaya of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Ema on hamaya|Ema on hamaya
File:Emado Hall of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Emado Hall|Emado Hall
File:Forest near Terrapin Pond of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Forest near Terrapin Pond|Forest near Terrapin Pond
File:Furosui Holy Spring of Kashii Shrine 2.jpg|alt=Furosui Holy Spring|Furosui Holy Spring
File:Furosui Holy Spring of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Furosui Holy Spring|Furosui Holy Spring
File:Furou spring water well.jpg|alt=Furou spring water well|Furou spring water well
File:Furousui01.jpg|alt=Furousui|Furousui
File:Gate and fence of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Gate and fence|Gate and fence
File:Guardian lion and stele for Ogitsuka in Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Guardian lion and stele|Guardian lion and stele
File:Guardian Lion in Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Guardian Lion|Guardian Lion
File:Guardian lions in Kashii Shrine 3.jpg|alt=Guardian lions|Guardian lions
File:Guardian lions in Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Guardian lions|Guardian lions
File:Haiden and honden of Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Haiden and honden|Haiden and honden
File:Haiden and Nakamon gate of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Haiden and Nakamon gate|Haiden and Nakamon gate
File:Haiden of Kashii Shrine 2.jpg|alt=Haiden|Haiden
File:Haiden of Kashii Shrine 20150426.JPG|alt=Haiden|Haiden
File:Haiden of Kashii Shrine 3.jpg|alt=Haiden|Haiden
File:Haiden of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Haiden|Haiden
File:Honden of Kashii Shrine 2.jpg|alt=Honden|Honden
File:Honden of Kashii Shrine 3.jpg|alt=Honden|Honden
File:Honden of Kashii Shrine 4.jpg|alt=Honden|Honden
File:Honden of Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Honden|Honden
File:Hydrangea macrophylla in Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Hydrangea macrophylla|Hydrangea macrophylla
File:Iris Pond of Kashii Shrine 1.jpg|alt=Iris Pond|Iris Pond
File:Torii of Kashii Shrine on Kashii-Sando Avenue 2.jpg|alt=Torii on Kashii-Sando Avenue|Torii on Kashii-Sando Avenue
File:Torii of Kashii Shrine on Kashii-Sando Avenue.jpg|alt=Torii on Kashii-Sando Avenue|Torii on Kashii-Sando Avenue
File:Torii of Kashii Shrine.JPG|alt=Torii|Torii
File:Torii on Kashii Sando Avenue.jpg|alt=Torii on Kashii Sando Avenue|Torii on Kashii Sando Avenue
File:Trees planted by Kikuko, Princess Takamatsu and Nobuhito, Prince Takamatsu in Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=Trees planted by royalty|Trees planted by royalty
File:View from Romon gate of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View from Romon gate|View from Romon gate
File:View in front of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View in front of shrine|View in front of shrine
File:View in Kashii Shrine 2.jpg|alt=View in Kashii Shrine|View in Kashii Shrine
File:View in Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View in Kashii Shrine|View in Kashii Shrine
File:View near Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View near Kashii Shrine|View near Kashii Shrine
File:View of Emado Hall of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of Emado Hall|View of Emado Hall
File:View of Furosui Holy Spring of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of Furosui Holy Spring|View of Furosui Holy Spring
File:View of Hayatsuji Shrine in Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of Hayatsuji Shrine|View of Hayatsuji Shrine
File:View of Iris Pond of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of Iris Pond|View of Iris Pond
File:View of Site of former Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of Site of former shrine|View of Site of former shrine
File:View of site of former Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of site of former shrine|View of site of former shrine
File:View of stele in Tongu Shrine of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of stele in Tongu Shrine|View of stele in Tongu Shrine
File:View of Terrapin Pond of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of Terrapin Pond|View of Terrapin Pond
File:View of torii in front of Romon Gate of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of torii in front of Romon Gate|View of torii in front of Romon Gate
File:View of torii of Kashii Shrine near Former Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View of torii near Former Kashii Shrine|View of torii near Former Kashii Shrine
File:View on Kabuto Mound of Kashii Shrine.jpg|alt=View on Kabuto Mound|View on Kabuto Mound
File:Warship Kashii Memorial.jpg|alt=Warship Kashii Memorial|Warship Kashii Memorial
File:香椎・万葉集歌碑.jpg|alt=Man'yōshū Monument|Man'yōshū Monument
</gallery>

==See also==
{{Commons Category|Kashii-gū}}
*{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}}

== References ==
<references /><!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->

}}
}}
}}
}}
}}

}}

}}
}}
}}
}}
}}
}}

{{stub}}
{{Hachiman Faith}}
{{Beppyo Shrines}}

\

{{translated page|ja|香椎宮|version=101846927|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{draft categories|{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|
{{draft categories|

[[en:Kashii-gū]]
[[fr:Kashii-gū]]
[[ja:香椎宮]]
[[fr:Kashii-gu]]
[[de:Kashii-gū]]
[[ja:Kashii-gū]]

<!--Kashii-gū--><!--Kashii-gū-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Kashii-gū}}
[[zh:香椎宮]]
<!--dewiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Kashii-gū}}
[[zh:香椎宮]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Kashii-gū}}
[[zh:香椎宮]]

{{wikidata link|Q631373}}
[[Category:Beppyo shrines]]
[[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuoka]]
[[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashi-ku, Fukuoka]]
[[Category:Chokusaisha]]
[[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]
[[Category:Edo period architecture]]
[[Category:History of Higashi Ward, Fukuoka City]]
[[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Fukuoka City]]
[[Category:Japan's Top 100 Exquisite Waters]]
[[Category:Japan–Korea Relations in Antiquity]]
[[Category:Kanpei Taisha]]
[[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]]
[[Category:Mausoleums in Japan]]
[[Category:Religion (Fukuoka)]]
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]]
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]]
[[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]]
[[Category:Springs of Japan]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
